title: Kernelについて、soul.mdもしくはphilosophy.mdを作るべきか？方法としてはobsidianにある情報を抽出して作成する。マルチエージェント・オーケストレーションで点数化して評価せよ
summary_text: - **Calibration drift (blocking if unmonitored):** deterministic scoring will harden early weighting errors; bad weights can repeatedly elevate the wrong beliefs while appearing “objective.” - **Coverage collapse in top-N:** top-N can over-select one theme/source cluster, suppressing minority but high-impact principles (safety/ethics edge cases). - **Tail audit can be statistically weak:** if tail sampling is too small or non-stratified, it won’t detect systematic misses; it becomes compliance theater. - **Determinism without input canonicalization is false determinism:** ordering/path/metadata variance can change rankings unless corpus normalization is strict. - **Governance gap:** scoring + review still needs a hard non-authority boundary (`AGENTS/CODEX/prompts` > `philosophy`) and fail-closed behavior when evidence quality/freshness is below threshold.
